title: A SpaceX/Tesla merger could trigger Musk’s $1T pay package automatically
summary:
SpaceX has a big IPO coming up, but the next step might be a merger of SpaceX and Tesla. And if so, it could be a backdoor to triggering Elon Musk’s trillion-dollar pay package automatically, without having to meet any of the operational milestones, thus diluting everyone’s shares without delivering on the promises that were made.

more…

title: Juiced Bikes now shipping its game-changing electric mopeds in the US
summary:
Juiced Bikes says its newly launched Scrambler e-bikes are now officially in stock and shipping, marking the next step in the brand’s return after its acquisition and relaunch under the team behind Lectric eBikes.

more…

title: Tesla’s ‘Full Self-Driving’ fraud lawsuit gets first hearing in China — 10 owners seek $583K
summary:
A Beijing court held its first hearing in a consumer fraud lawsuit against Tesla over its “Full Self-Driving” software, with 10 owners seeking more than 3.95 million yuan ($583,000) in damages.

The case, which we first covered when it was filed last September, has grown from 7 to 10 plaintiffs and marks China’s first collective legal challenge targeting Tesla’s FSD promises.

more…

title: CT keeps home solar incentive alive through 2035, batteries are the big winners
summary:
A new bill extending home and community solar incentive programs has passed both houses of the Connecticut Senate, and just needs Governor Ned Lamont’s signature to make it into law. It’s taken us a while to parse through it, but one thing is clear: batteries are the big winners.

more…

title: We could all learn from Australia’s brewing movement to tax gas
summary:
During a global moment where oil prices are spiking tremendously, a movement to increase gas taxes is brewing in Australia. And if it does succeed, it could provide a template that could help other countries not only fund their own prosperity, but reduce the tremendous damage done by fossil fuels.

more…

title: Fortescue Nabrawind deploy first crane-less wind turbine in Africa
summary:
Fortescue‘s recently-acquired turbine subsidiary Nabrawind has done something that was believed to be impossible: they’ve installed a full-scale, energy producing wind turbine in harsh Namibian conditions using a new, crane-less deployment process.

more…

title: Tourists can experience Athabasca Glacier in this first-ever ELECTRIC Ice Explorer [video]
summary:
Built to allow up to 52 guests to explore the frozen wilderness of Alberta’s Jasper National Park in serene, environmentally responsible silence, the new Electric Ice Explorer joining Pursuit Banff’s Columbia Icefield Adventure fleet is a machine that’s packed with firsts.

more…

title: This Tesla owner won $10k in court for Tesla’s FSD lies (Updated)
summary:
For over a decade now, Tesla has sold a promise of vehicles that can drive themselves, even stating that every car it produced had all the hardware for self-driving.

But after years of the company being unable to deliver, some owners want their money back. Ben Gawiser is one of those owners, who recently won a $10,600 judgment due to Tesla’s failure to deliver. But Tesla kept fighting to delay payment, even just a few days at a time.

(5/29 Update, original post 5/2: He finally got his money back. This article has been updated with new information below)

more…

title: Blink and Kempower expand US East Coast EV fast charging
summary:
Kempower and Blink Charging are expanding their EV fast-charging partnership across the US East Coast, with 14 new charging sites planned through 2026.

more…

title: The 2027 Chevy Equinox and Blazer EVs fix one big complaint drivers had
summary:
The 2027 Chevy Equinox EV and Blazer EV are almost here, bringing a few updates that fix some of the biggest complaints with the current models.

summary: Tesla’s top engineer says he thinks about a tri-motor Model 3 “all the time,” but the company’s priorities lie elsewhere.

title: China Is Turning Against The EV Gimmicks It Helped Popularize
summary: Hidden handles, giant screens, yokes, and reclining seats make Chinese EVs feel futuristic. Now regulators are questioning their safety.

title: What EV Slowdown? Plug-In Cars Made Up 25% Of Car Sales Last Year
summary: The U.S. is getting left behind on electrification, a new report says. The rest of the world is charging ahead in 2026.

title: I Saw Ferrari’s First EV In The Flesh. Here’s My Honest Take
summary: The Ferrari Luce is here. The company's first EV costs 550,000 euros ($640,000) and looks like no Ferrari before it.

summary: The first-generation VinFast VF 8 had a good price, but failed in just about every other category. Now, there’s a new one.
